atomic_claims.text: "嫖"最初读一声（piāo），意为"灵动洒脱、勇健轻捷"
汉武帝时期名将霍去病曾被封为"嫖姚校尉"，取"勇健轻捷"之意
汉文帝的女儿馆陶公主名叫"刘嫖"，名字里的"嫖"是美好寓意
"妓、娼"最初写作"伎、倡"，指唱歌跳舞的艺人
"伎、倡"是朝廷官方认可的职业，入职难度堪比现代文工团
为区分男女艺人，才将"伎、倡"换成女字旁的"妓、娼"
古代诗词中常出现的"歌妓"实则是对有才艺女性的称呼
"妖"最初形容女子美好、容貌艳丽，并非指"妖艳、妖精"
《说文解字》中解释"妖，妍也"，即美丽的意思
"媚"的原意是"婀娜多姿、惹人喜爱"
《诗经》中"媚兹一人"表达对人的喜爱
"媛"最初指女子友善美好、才华出众
"名媛"本是对有身份、有才华女性的尊称
这些女字旁汉字最初都是"满满的赞美之意"
这些字含义的演变反映了不同时代的文化观念变化
为何偏偏是这些原本赞美女性的字被"污名化"——背后隐藏着更深层的社会文化心理
这些字千百年间"从褒义沦为贬义"是确定的历史事实
"嫖"字如今成了低俗词汇
"妓、娼"换用女字旁"本质上并无贬义"
"媛"被贴上了"走捷径"的标签，出现"假名媛"等说法

institutional_attributions.name: 汉武帝 / 霍去病
institutional_attributions.context: Historical figures cited to support the etymology of "嫖"

institutional_attributions.name: 汉文帝 / 馆陶公主刘嫖
institutional_attributions.context: Historical figures cited as evidence that "嫖" was a positive term

institutional_attributions.name: 《说文解字》
institutional_attributions.context: Ancient dictionary cited as authority for the original meaning of "妖"

institutional_attributions.name: 《诗经》
institutional_attributions.context: Classic text cited as evidence for the positive meaning of "媚"

emotional_hooks.type: victimization framing with strong moral charge

affected_groups.basis: The entire article is about female-radical characters and implicitly about women's social status as reflected in language
The article makes etymological and historical claims that implicate the field of Chinese philology

affected_groups.group: 名为相关汉字的人 (People whose names contain these characters)
affected_groups.basis: The article mentions that people with "嫖" in their name would be mocked
The article discusses the historical status of 妓/娼 as court-sanctioned entertainers

category_definitions.significance: The female radical in Chinese characters; the article's central organizing concept
Core framing concept — the article claims these characters were deliberately or systematically devalued
The evaluative polarity the article uses to trace semantic drift
The linguistic phenomenon the article claims is at work
First case study; claimed to mean 'brisk and agile' originally
Second case study; claimed to derive from 伎/倡 meaning 'entertainer'
Third case study; claimed to originally mean 'beautiful' per Shuowen Jiezi
Fourth case study; claimed to originally mean 'graceful and charming'
Fifth case study; claimed to originally mean 'talented and beautiful woman'

unknowns: Whether the Shuowen Jiezi actually glosses 妖 as 妍也, or if this is a misattribution
Whether the original meaning of 嫖 as 'brisk/agile' is well-attested in classical sources or is a folk etymology
Whether the semantic drift of these characters was specifically gender-motivated or part of broader linguistic processes
Whether the claim that 伎/倡 were changed to 妓/娼 specifically to distinguish male and female entertainers is historically accurate
Whether 馆陶公主's name 刘嫖 used 嫖 in the 'brisk' sense or another sense
The actual timeline of semantic change for each character
Whether the 'stigmatization' framing oversimplifies complex philological histories
Whether the article's implication of deliberate/systematic devaluation of female-radical characters is supported by evidence
